Appears to be for sourcing only , previously iSupplier, iRisk & CLM were included. Also appears that a UAE based support resource is mandatory. Not confident this will be a deal that meets our min criteria, but we'll respond to the PQQ and review the RFP (if successful) and decide to participate or not. 13th Mar - CPO unresponsive for some time, not clear if project shelved or lost (last update that it was delayed) - moving back to initial interest and out of Q1 - will continue to nudge for confirmation one way or another. 14th Jan - No response to follow ups, reducing confidence level, but will keep trying, may just be the consultant report delayed - worst case should see the CPO at the Dubai event end Jan. 12th Dec - No update yet, moving back to Qualified and Q2-26 until we know more. 13th Nov - Spoke to CPO at the 100CPO event in Dubai, has been some delay while a consultant reviews the internal review (see below) no timelines yet. 26th Oct - Finally received a response to the update requests.... "First and foremost, we would like to apologize for the delay in providing an update on the status of this project, as we have been undertaking an internal review and further evaluating the requirements and proposed solutions. At present, a reputed third-party consultant has been engaged to carry out a comprehensive analysis, given the critical nature of the requirement. Once the report is concluded, we will revert back to you at the earliest." ... believe the consultant is Efficio - will move the close date back to end Q4 until we know more. 9th Oct - Reached out to PC and CPO, Tanya in town, would they like to meet up for a live demo or informal meet over a coffee, no response to several nudges. 10th Sept - Ahmed CPO attended the Dubai World Tour event, got a quick update that they are expecting a consultant report to land soon, on the direction of procurement and will move toward POC demos on modules not yet covered (Contract Mgt, iSupplier etc.) over the coming months with a decision by end of the year ....moved close date back to end Dec. 19th August - SPOC returned from holiday yesterday - most of team still off, some internal stakeholder reviews still underway, expecting a decision in c 4weeks he mentioned some positive comments, but also acknowledged some of our workarounds to meet their (unusual) process, consciously keeping the confidence level low. 23rd July - Have been nudging for a sync - confirmed SPOC Mohamad is on holiday until 9th August. 11th July - requested more info on reporting and screen shots, provided by Anuradha. 3rd July - ARM lead accessed the showpad and downloaded the documents/videos 2nd July - Despite the urgency still not accessed the Show Pad yet, not a great sign - sent reminders 1st July - They haven't accessed the showpad yet, but requested further screenshots by EOB latest, Sent. 30th June - Sent through their requirements (videos, questionnaire, supporting documents) via Showpad (video files are huge) 20th June - Sent through another step, in addition to a Sourcing video covering their specific (and very odd) process (after a live demo and 2 x live POC sessions), also want the same for CLM. 16th June - Re: demo of live proof of concept held on 12th, with mixed success, (Ana had to dial in again due to travel/visa issues) they have a very unusual process they expect us to follow and our fit is probably 70-80%, some gaps we can cover, some we can't/won't - next step is to provide a video of the process so they can review, reducing Confidence level to 10% - if the video is not well received we should consider qualifying out. 2nd June - RE-demo scheduled for 12th June, also sent comprehensive screenshot deck (1st June) to give confidence that we can meet their requirements but were unlucky due to random demo site issues. 29th May - POC held with Dan (onsite) and Anuradha dialling in (couldn't attend due to travel issues) - didn't go well, problems with site availability (also some functionality gaps) didn't give a good impression, but its rescuable - they agreed to a follow up/re-demo session on 12th, following up with Screenshots also to show how it should have worked..... if we have issues again it will be dead, hoping to get Ana to Dubai for a F2F session. 21st May - Formal POC/Conference Room Pilot scheduled for 29th May - DQ & Anuradha will be onsite in Dubai 28/4 Moved to Formal Evaluation, however stated decision timescales makes Q1 very unlikely, moved to Q2 17/4 Demo held (DQ onsite, Deepesh couldn't get a visa so dialled in ... • Across 6,519 chars of NextStep covering 20+ dated entries from May 2024 through Mar 2026 ‚Äî including disco calls, World Tour engagement, PQQ submission, consultant (likely Efficio) review ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, GenAI or autonomous. Product scope contains no AI SKUs (iContract; iSource; iSupplier; iRequest). The 27 Mar 2026 rep note: 'Clarifications received - they are emphasising local support requirements... if local support is a showstopper we will qualify out.'
• On 16 Mar 2026: 'PQQ received today - with Eid it only gives us 2.5 working days before deadline 2pm Mon 23rd Mar. Appears to be for sourcing only, previously iSupplier, iRisk & CLM were included.' Scope is shrinking to sourcing only; the entire procurement evaluation is run by a third-party consultant ('a reputed third-party consultant has been engaged to carry out a comprehensive analysis'). No AI language in any clarification or buyer correspondence captured.
• Confidence is Medium given the unusually rich NextStep (6,519 chars over ~22 months). The deal has been narrowed to sourcing-only, mandates UAE-local support, and is consultant-mediated ‚Äî none of which selects for AI capability. Score 2 reflects the complete absence of AI products in scope and AI language in dialogue.

metrics.narrative: Mohamed Arshed quantified the scale of the pain in the 28 Nov 2024 introductory call: roughly $300M in annual spend managed by a team of up to 12 people entirely through Oracle Fusion with 'no sourcing portal' and manual, unscored technical/commercial evaluation. No dollar-value ROI or savings target has ever been stated by the buyer in any call or written exchange; the case remains scale-based rather than monetized (source: Avoma intro call, 28 Nov 2024). Quantifying the cost of continued manual process against that $300M base is a live opportunity, not yet taken.

champion.narrative: Mohamed Arshed self-initiated the engagement (2 Jan 2024/2025 emails requesting demos), personally drove both live POC sessions (29 May and 12 Jun 2025), and is named champion in MEDDPICC 2.0. However, he has not personally replied to any correspondence since the 14 May 2026 update; PQQ and NDA execution since then has run through operational contacts Ahmad Jan and Krishna Prasad, with no confirmed champion-level push behind the stalled RFP. What developed: he drove the deal to two POCs and a PQQ; what is missing: any personal engagement or advocacy in the last ~8 weeks.

competition.narrative: Coupa was named directly by Mohamed Arshed in the 28 Nov 2024 call ('give me a little comparison on Coupa'), where Zycus positioned its owner-led stability against Coupa's private-equity ownership; no further Coupa mention since. JAGGAER is listed in CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 as the current eSourcing incumbent, though the 28 Nov 2024 call stated 'there is no sourcing portal; buyers manage the entire process manually' — an unresolved contradiction worth clarifying with the buyer. GEP Worldwide appears only in the SF Competitors__c field with no call corroboration. With no new competitor evidence surfaced this sweep, the most credible current threat is inertia: a stalled, unlaunched RFP and a cooling champion favor status quo/do-nothing over any named rival.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain was articulated in detail in the 28 Nov 2024 call: 'there are a lot of gaps that does not fulfill our business needs and as per process compliance' with no sourcing portal, missing technical/commercial scoring, limited document submission tracking, and no supplier performance management. Mohamed Arshed named himself the pain owner ('we found your software to be covering all of those pain points'). The pain has not been re-articulated verbally in the last 90 days but was reconfirmed structurally through the detailed Mar-Apr 2026 PQQ functional-gap clarifications.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting has not begun; the deal has not passed the PQQ/RFP stage. The only paper-process activity to date is the vendor NDA, routed by Benita Nayana Ramos on 8 Apr 2026 via DocuSign for the deal owner's manager to sign. MSA/SOW terms, procurement channel, and legal review have not been discussed. Visibility will only emerge once the formal RFP is issued and Zycus is shortlisted.

economic_buyer.narrative: Ahmed Raafat (Head of Procurement and Supply Chain) is named as economic buyer/budget owner in MEDDPICC 2.0 and attended the 17 Apr 2025 demo, which Next_Step__c describes as 'well received by the 3 decision makers (inc CPO).' Since that demo, he has only been cc'd on the PQQ/NDA correspondence thread (through 8 Apr 2026) and two rep-initiated content shares (8 and 19 May 2026), with no personal reply logged in any channel. The EB is identified but passive; his direct sign-off on budget for the now sourcing-only scope has never been confirmed in writing.

decision_process.narrative: The observed process is PQQ (received 16 Mar 2026, submitted 26 Mar, clarified through 8 Apr) -> vendor registration/NDA -> formal RFP (drafted, not yet launched as of 14 May/7 Jul 2026) -> evaluation/qualify-in-or-out -> decision. Vendor registration and NDA routing (handled by Benita Nayana Ramos, requiring sign-off from the deal owner's manager via DocuSign, 8 Apr 2026) ran in parallel to PQQ clarifications, showing a structured, consultant-influenced, multi-stage procurement process. The process is currently stalled at the RFP-launch step.

decision_criteria.narrative: Four criteria were stated directly by Mohamed Arshed in the 28 Nov 2024 call: AI-assisted efficiency ('as much as we can make our life easier'), mandatory Oracle Fusion integration, a proper technical/commercial scoring and compliance system, and clarity on data-hosting location. The Mar-Apr 2026 PQQ added detailed functional criteria (dual-envelope tender segregation, EOI/NDA workflow continuity, audit trails) confirmed point-by-point in Zycus's 8 Apr 2026 written response, e.g. 'the Commercial envelope is locked and unable to view until the Technical envelope evaluation is completed.'

day_summary.overall: After months of silence since the April PQQ clarification exchange, Zycus resurfaced this deal only because Dan Quinn - the rep who owned it - has left the company. His replacement, Mohamad Alhakim, sent a re-introduction email to Ahmad Jan and Ahmed Raafat at A.R.M. Holding, claiming the SE has kept him briefed and asking to schedule a call in the next two weeks to pick things back up. The ball is now in A.R.M.'s court to respond and confirm a slot, with the deal effectively needing to be re-started from a standing stop.

hard.ais_why:
• Across 6,519 chars of NextStep covering 20+ dated entries from May 2024 through Mar 2026 ‚Äî including disco calls, World Tour engagement, PQQ submission, consultant (likely Efficio) review ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, GenAI or autonomous. Product scope contains no AI SKUs (iContract; iSource; iSupplier; iRequest). The 27 Mar 2026 rep note: 'Clarifications received - they are emphasising local support requirements... if local support is a showstopper we will qualify out.'
• On 16 Mar 2026: 'PQQ received today - with Eid it only gives us 2.5 working days before deadline 2pm Mon 23rd Mar. Appears to be for sourcing only, previously iSupplier, iRisk & CLM were included.' Scope is shrinking to sourcing only; the entire procurement evaluation is run by a third-party consultant ('a reputed third-party consultant has been engaged to carry out a comprehensive analysis'). No AI language in any clarification or buyer correspondence captured.
• Confidence is Medium given the unusually rich NextStep (6,519 chars over ~22 months). The deal has been narrowed to sourcing-only, mandates UAE-local support, and is consultant-mediated ‚Äî none of which selects for AI capability. Score 2 reflects the complete absence of AI products in scope and AI language in dialogue.
